marker_results.evidence: Sessions include uphill power intervals on trail climbs, trail-specific strength (split squats, step-downs, single-leg calf raises), long trail run with climbing practice, and mobility work for ankle/hip stability on uneven terrain.
marker_results.improvement_hint: None needed - excellent trail-specific adaptations throughout.

soft_assessment.primary_goal: to become a faster trail runner

strengths: Highly trail-specific programming with hill intervals for uphill power, trail durability strength work, and climbing economy focus that directly supports the goal of becoming a faster trail runner
Excellent load management that reduces weekly hours from 11.3 to 8.2 while strategically adding quality sessions during transition from cycling-heavy training
Exceptional detail and specificity in session descriptions with concrete RPE targets, terrain guidance, and specific exercises that make the plan highly actionable
Thoughtful risk management that acknowledges moderate overreach risk and missing sleep data with appropriate adaptations and clear monitoring guidance
suggested_improvements: Frame the cycling session more explicitly as supporting trail running goals, such as 'maintain aerobic base to support trail running intervals later this week'
Add specific bail-out protocols for quality sessions beyond general RPE guidance, such as 'if you can't complete rep 3 at target effort, reduce remaining reps to 4 min each'
Provide more specific terrain guidance for the long trail run regarding technical difficulty and grade steepness to optimize the climbing economy benefits
Consider adding approximate pace or power targets for the interval session to complement the RPE guidance for more precise execution
soft_assessment.summary: Excellent trail running plan that effectively transitions from recent cycling-heavy training to running-focused work while managing moderate overreach risk. Strong trail-specific elements and detailed execution guidance.
